allowed_public_wording: The cleaned survey contains 212 complete responses, of which 172 are SMEs; 39 large firms are retained only as a comparator and one case lacks firm-size information.
In the SME-only engaged sample, 31.4% report active AI use and 61.0% report either active use or projects in planning, testing or deployment.
forbidden_wording: 31.4% of all Romanian SMEs use AI.

allowed_public_wording: Cost and technical expertise are the dominant reported capability constraints among surveyed SMEs, selected by 87.2% and 79.7%, respectively.
forbidden_wording: Cost and expertise are statistically proven causes of non-adoption.

allowed_public_wording: Most surveyed SMEs report at least one positive workforce-preparation measure, but no-specific-measures responses are not the complement of positive upskilling because the item was multiple-response.
forbidden_wording: Only 22.1% of SMEs have no upskilling.

allowed_public_wording: Public-support demand is high in the engaged SME sample: 98.3% describe government support as very or moderately important.
forbidden_wording: Romanian SMEs unanimously demand public intervention.

allowed_public_wording: Association tests suggest a firm-size gradient in the broader active-or-planning frontier, but these results are exploratory and non-causal.
status: allowed if effect sizes and caveats are reported

allowed_public_wording: A parsimonious SME-only logit is admissible only as supplementary robustness; the main article should rely primarily on descriptive and cross-tabulated evidence.
forbidden_wording: The model identifies causal determinants of AI adoption.
